title: 4. Validation Processes > 4.3 Test Case Management and Approvals
text_content: Test Cases shall be created, reviewed, and approved prior to execution per the Validation W ork Instruction (1), ADLM Guidance (12), and RACI for Design and Development (15). Test records shall be reviewed by a second individual (Validation Manager, Validation Lead, peer Validation Specialist, Product Manager or QA). The approval of the Validation Summary Report/MRD by the Validation representative shall confirm that the test records have received this second review. The documented test process for validation consists of the Test Design Strategy and modular test cases traced to User Needs and acceptance criterion. Selected test cases may trace to more than one user need according to the clinical workflow. Each test case is designed so that the input is targeted at a high-level clinical workflow action and does not state specifically how the user must execute the action. Test records shall be reviewed by a second individual (Validation Manager, Validation Lead, peer Validation Specialist, Product Manager or QA). The approval of the Validation Summary Report/MRD by the Validation representative shall confirm that the test records have received this second review. The documented test process for validation consists of the Test Design Strategy and modular test cases traced to User Needs and acceptance criterion. Selected test cases may trace to more than one user need according to the clinical workflow. Each test case is designed so that the input is targeted at a high-level clinical workflow action and does not state specifically how the user must execute the action. title: 2. Validation Test Execution Overview > 2.3 Test Case Management > 2.3.4 Documentation of Test Objective Evidence > 2.3.4.1 Early Validation Testing (Continued)
text_content: For test case execution prior to Validation TRR, a subset of approved test cases will be executed in Test Management Tool. This is known as Dry Run. Objective evidence documentation is the Test Management Tool is not required. Test Results will capture a Pass, Fail, Blocked or Not Complete status. Development DRs will be submitted in ADLM ADO. Document and/or test case error will result in a Not Complete status. Actual results will record the corrective measure. Tester will correct the test case problem, reapprove and re- execute it. Findings from Early Validation Testing rounds may be captured in a review report and dispositioned by the project core team. Objective evidence may be documented in the review report.  Each finding shall be associated to a Product Feedback (PF) or a DR. For a summary of the Early Validation activities performed and test results refer to the Validation dry run test plan, Halcyon 4.0 MR1 Validation Dry Run (Suite ID: 1292269)

title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.1     Purpose
text_content: The purpose of this document is to identify the product-specific standards, laws, and/or directives for product compliance, safety, and performance that apply to the product. This form is intended to create a record which indicates the reports needed for market access submissions which include, but are not limited to, the International Submissions Dossier (ISD) file.

text_content: Halcyon 2.0: An additional CNSC (Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission) application must be developed and submitted close to the time of the Health Canada submission, per SOR/200-205, Section 10 of the Nuclear Safety and Control Act; section 1 of this act presents Definitions (“certificate”, “Class II prescribed equipment”, etc.). Anticipated time to receive the necessary certificate is approximatgely 4 months. The Varian Palo Alto Radiation Safety group develops and submits the CNSC application.The CNSC application package is sent immediately after the Health Canada Medical Device license submission package is sent. The documents required are taken from the ISD, with additional documents developed by the Varian Palo Alto Radiation Safety group.
